title: Supporting context for translation performance
prose: What supporting context — beyond the bare term — maximises EN->KO translation quality? Candidates from SNOMED itself: the semantic tag (always present, cheap disambiguation), the parent / is-a hierarchy (sense + domain), synonyms, and text definitions (rich but only ~2.4% of concepts). Plus the existing levers: retrieval exemplars and the style guide. This is the TRANSLATION-PERFORMANCE arm of the project — reduce errors at the source — distinct from the confidence thread (output-translations-confidence), which PRIORITISES review of what remains. Both serve the root goal of minimising terminologist effort, and they interact: better context lowers the error rate, and some context (e.g. detected ambiguity) may itself feed a confidence signal.
